Courses in 2025-2026 Academic Catalog - DRAFT COPY

COMM459 Special Topics in Science Communication (3 Credits) This seminar course is designed to help students learn a variety of topics of science communication. Repeatable to: 12 credits if content differs.

COMM482 Intercultural Communication (3 Credits) The major variables of communication in an intercultural context: cultural, racial and national differences; stereotypes; values; cultural assumptions; and verbal and nonverbal channels.

COMM460 Public Life in American Communities, 1634-1900 (3 Credits) Ways that Americans have used their voice to create public life. Focus is on the diverse social communities that have characterized American life and the place and characteristics of oral discourse in each.

COMM461 Voices of Public Leadership in the Twentieth Century (3 Credits) Study of the use of speaking in the power struggles of the twentieth century. Focus is on important speakers of the century, their social and policy influence, and the struggle to expand the diversity of voices with power in the public sphere.
